global_summary: 10 projects tracked with 90+ runs. latent-mage/tok-gen is the most active with 13 running jobs and promising FID results (best 2.58). Most robotics/video planning runs crash consistently, suggesting infrastructure or config issues across those projects.
attention_summary: 8A100-NSG-NOISE10 has catastrophic FID=389; likely broken config—kill to free GPUs. jacobian-learning has 18 runs with most crashing at epoch 0—systemic setup bug. All better-world-model/large_video_planner runs crash despite 100+ GPU-hours invested.

projects.project_insight: Most active project with 13 running jobs and strong results. 4H100-NOSEP-3-S03 achieved remarkable FID=2.58 (finished). FLOWABL-SEP models achieve FID~3.2-3.6 with separate flow formulation. NSG-NOSHIFT variants are strong at FID~5.2. NOISE10 is catastrophically broken (FID=389). New 8A100_40 runs just started and showing early promise. The D16 depth variant underperforms D32/D48.

runs.name: 8A100-NSG-NOISE0
runs.signal_reason: NOISE0 config has FID=25.6—5x worse than NOSHIFT baseline
runs.viz_insight: Total loss high and volatile (0.30-0.48); flow loss stable but model struggles
runs.run_insight: Zero-noise variant achieves only FID=25.6 after 15h on 8xA100. The noise-free formulation clearly hurts generation quality vs NOSHIFT (FID=5.25). Consider stopping to save compute.

runs.name: 8A100-NSG-NOISE10
runs.signal_reason: NOISE10 has catastrophic FID=389—completely broken, waste of 8xA100
runs.viz_insight: FID=389 indicates model generates garbage
runs.run_insight: NOISE10 variant is catastrophically broken with FID=389 after 15h on 8xA100. This run should be killed immediately to free resources. The noise level of 10 appears to completely destroy training signal.

runs.name: 8A100-FLOWABL-SEP-7-D32
runs.signal_reason: FLOWABL-SEP-7 achieves FID=3.16—best among running jobs
runs.viz_insight: High raw loss (~56-81) but FID is excellent; scale-adaptive loss is working
runs.run_insight: FLOWABL-SEP-7-D32 is the best running model at FID=3.16 after 14h on 8xA100. The high raw loss (~70) contrasts with excellent FID, suggesting the scale-adaptive loss formulation is effective. cfg_scale=4.8 with interval 0.1 is optimal.

runs.name: 4H100-NOSEP-3-S03
runs.signal_reason: Best FID=2.58 in entire project—NOSEP-3 is the winner
runs.viz_insight: FID=2.58 with cfg_scale=2.6; excellent generation quality
runs.run_insight: NOSEP-3-S03 achieves the best FID=2.58 across all tok-gen runs, finishing in 4.7h on 4xH100. cfg_scale=2.6 is moderate, and rfid-val=1.48 confirms quality. This is the current SOTA for this project.

runs.name: 4H100-NSG-NOSHIFT
runs.signal_reason: 4H100 NOSHIFT at FID=5.23, running 20h with stable loss trend
runs.viz_insight: Loss/total fluctuates 0.26-0.32 but flow loss stable ~0.04; converging
runs.run_insight: Running 20h on 4xH100. FID=5.23 with cfg_scale=1.8. Flow loss trend is flat ~0.04, suggesting near convergence. Good baseline for NSG approach.

runs.name: 8A100-NSG-D48
runs.signal_reason: D48 at FID=5.94, between D24 (6.15) and D32 (5.25)
runs.viz_insight: Flow loss trend ~0.035-0.043; moderate fluctuation
runs.run_insight: Depth 48 model at FID=5.94 after 15h. Surprisingly worse than NOSHIFT D32 (5.25)—deeper is not always better. Diminishing returns or underfitting at this depth.

runs.name: 8A100-NSG-D24
runs.signal_reason: D24 variant at FID=6.15, flow loss trending down
runs.viz_insight: Flow loss at 0.75-0.875 bin dropping (0.074→0.040); high-freq improving
runs.run_insight: 8A100 D24 at FID=6.15 after 15h. Flow loss trend shows improvement especially in high-frequency bins. Depth 24 underperforms depth 32 (FID 5.25 vs 6.15).

runs.name: 8A100-NSG-NOSHIFT
runs.signal_reason: 8A100 NOSHIFT matches 4H100 at FID=5.25, validating scaling
runs.viz_insight: Loss trend stable; flow loss ~0.035-0.048 with slight fluctuation
runs.run_insight: 8A100 NOSHIFT achieves FID=5.25, nearly identical to 4H100 variant (5.23). Confirms NOSHIFT approach is robust across hardware. Running 15h.

runs.name: 16A100-DEBUG-flow-14-dit-l
runs.signal_reason: 16A100 DiT-L debug run early stage, loss trending down
runs.viz_insight: Loss/total decreasing: 23.9→18.7→23.3; noisy but downward trend
runs.run_insight: Large-scale debug run on 16xA100 after 10.5h. Loss=20.9 is still high but trending down. No FID evaluation yet. The flow-14 DiT-L architecture needs more training time.

runs.name: 8A100-NSG-D16
runs.signal_reason: D16 at FID=8.62—significantly worse than D32/D48, too shallow
runs.viz_insight: Best cfg_scale=2.6 (higher than D32's 1.8) suggests underfitting
runs.run_insight: Depth 16 achieves only FID=8.62. The higher optimal cfg_scale (2.6 vs 1.8) indicates the shallower model needs more guidance. Clear evidence that D16 has insufficient capacity.

runs.name: 4H100-FLOWABL-SEP-3-D32
runs.signal_reason: FLOWABL-SEP-3 crashed but achieved FID=3.58 in 4.2h
runs.viz_insight: FID=3.58 at cfg_scale=6.8; high cfg indicates learned sharpness
runs.run_insight: 4H100 FLOWABL-SEP-3-D32 achieved FID=3.58 before crashing at 4.2h. High cfg_scale (6.8) compared to SEP-7 (4.8). The newer SEP-7 run beats this at FID=3.16.

runs.name: 8A100_40-NSG-NOISE0
runs.signal_reason: New 8A100_40 NOISE0 run at 2.5h, flow loss ~0.024
runs.viz_insight: Flow loss trend ~0.02-0.04; early but promising
runs.run_insight: Fresh NOISE0 variant on A100-40GB, running 2.5h. Flow loss=0.024 is early but lower than the older 8A100 NOISE0 at similar stage. Too early to judge FID.

runs.name: 8A100_40-NSG-D16
runs.signal_reason: New D16 on A100_40 already at FID=8.51 in 2.5h
runs.viz_insight: FID=8.51 matches 8A100 D16 (8.62); consistent across hardware
runs.run_insight: A100-40GB D16 achieves FID=8.51 in just 2.5h, matching the 15h 8A100 result (8.62). Suggests D16 converges fast but to a limited FID.

runs.name: 8A100_40-NSG-NOSHIFT
runs.signal_reason: New 8A100_40 NOSHIFT at 2.5h, flow loss ~0.039
runs.viz_insight: Flow loss trend stable ~0.039-0.046; on track for NOSHIFT baseline
runs.run_insight: Fresh NOSHIFT on A100-40GB. Flow loss=0.039 at 2.5h is consistent with 8A100 NOSHIFT at similar stage. Expected to converge to similar FID~5.2.

runs.name: 8A100_40-NSG-BASE
runs.signal_reason: Just started NSG-BASE on A100_40, 0.09h in
runs.viz_insight: Very early; flow loss=0.007 with 4 data points showing stability
runs.run_insight: Just launched NSG-BASE on 8xA100-40GB. Only 0.09h in with flow_loss=0.007. Too early to assess.

runs.name: 8A100_40-NSG-SHIFT03
runs.signal_reason: SHIFT03 crashed at 3.7h with flow_loss=0.036
runs.viz_insight: Flow loss=0.036 at crash; was training normally
runs.run_insight: NSG-SHIFT03 variant ran 3.7h before crashing. Flow loss=0.036 was reasonable. No FID was evaluated before crash.

runs.name: 4H100-FLOWABL-SEP-3-D32
runs.signal_reason: FLOWABL-SEP-3 crashed at 1.15h before FID eval
runs.viz_insight: Loss=42.6; early training with high raw loss expected for FLOWABL
runs.run_insight: 4H100 FLOWABL-SEP-3-D32 crashed after 1.15h. Loss=42.6 is expected for early FLOWABL training. Needs restart.

runs.name: 8A100_40-NSG-BASE
runs.signal_reason: NSG-BASE crashed at 1h—early predecessor
runs.viz_insight: Flow loss=0.004; very early training
runs.run_insight: First NSG-BASE attempt, crashed at 1h. Multiple restarts have been needed for this config.

runs.name: 8A100_40-NSG-BASE
runs.signal_reason: NSG-BASE crashed at 0.87h—predecessor to rwiq1rzy
runs.viz_insight: Flow loss=0.012; early stage before crash
runs.run_insight: Previous NSG-BASE attempt on A100-40GB. Crashed at 0.87h. The new run rwiq1rzy appears to be the retry.

runs.name: 4A100_40-NSG-NOISE0
runs.signal_reason: Failed at launch with only param counts logged
runs.viz_insight: No training data; 218M params logged
runs.run_insight: Setup failure. Only logged model size (218M params). Config or resource issue.

projects.project_insight: Both runs crashed within ~20 minutes. The 14B model (loss=0.042) and 1.3B ego model (loss=0.107) both terminated early, suggesting environment or OOM issues rather than training problems. Consider debugging the crash cause before relaunching.

runs.name: stack_d0d1_dual_N4_M4_14B_20260303_0816 (2026-03-03/08-17-18)
runs.signal_reason: Crashed after only 0.36h and 449 steps with 14B model
runs.viz_insight: Too few steps to establish trend
runs.run_insight: 14B model reached loss=0.042 in 449 steps before crashing. Likely OOM given the model size. Very short runtime wasted.

runs.name: stack_d0d1_ego_N4_M4_1.3B_20260302_1952 (2026-03-02/19-52-58)
runs.signal_reason: Crashed after 0.33h, 1.3B ego model at loss=0.107
runs.viz_insight: Too few steps to assess convergence
runs.run_insight: 1.3B ego model reached epoch 5 and loss=0.107 in 324 steps. Crashed quickly—likely same infrastructure issue as the 14B run.

projects.project_insight: The allegro_flow_mv long run achieved excellent loss=0.0026 (val=0.0028) over 19K steps, showing strong convergence. The newer mixture_sim run crashed early at 1.4h but already reached loss=0.016. Two short allegro runs appear to be failed restarts. Focus on resuming the mixture_sim experiment.

runs.name: train_wan_decoder_mixture_sim (2026-03-09/02-23-55)
runs.signal_reason: Promising mixture_sim run crashed early at 1.4h—resume needed
runs.viz_insight: Loss=0.016, val=0.020 after only 974 steps; converging fast
runs.run_insight: Mixture sim decoder reached loss=0.016 with val_loss=0.020 in just 974 steps. Grad norm=1.55 is healthy. Crashed too early—high priority to restart.

runs.name: train_wan_decoder_allegro_flow_mv (2026-03-04/15-01-31)
runs.signal_reason: Best in project: loss=0.0026, val=0.0028 over 19K steps
runs.viz_insight: Fully converged; near-zero train-val gap
runs.run_insight: Allegro flow multiview decoder trained 17.8h to loss=0.0026. Excellent convergence with minimal overfitting (val=0.0028). This is the reference checkpoint for downstream use.

runs.name: train_wan_decoder_allegro_flow_mv (2026-03-04/14-16-03)
runs.signal_reason: Short run crashed at 0.59h, 449 steps
runs.viz_insight: Loss=0.014 at step 449; LR reached 0.0001
runs.run_insight: Slightly longer than the other failed restart but still crashed early at 449 steps. Loss=0.014 was reasonable before termination.

runs.name: train_wan_decoder_allegro_flow_mv (2026-03-04/14-38-30)
runs.signal_reason: Failed restart—crashed after 0.21h with LR still at 0
runs.viz_insight: LR=0 suggests warmup never completed
runs.run_insight: Only 224 steps before crash. LR=0 indicates the warmup schedule hadn't kicked in. Wasted compute.

projects.project_insight: Two long runs both crashed. The flow_dual_stable_bg model (loss=0.023, val=0.025) shows good convergence with small train-val gap. The wan_dual model (loss=0.043, val=0.029) ran longer but with higher loss. Crashes after 11-16h suggest timeout or SLURM limits.

runs.name: mg13b_flow_dual_20260305_225531_stable_bg (runs/mg13b_flow_dual_20260305_225531_stable_bg)
runs.signal_reason: Strong convergence (loss=0.023) but crashed at 11.2h—needs restart
runs.viz_insight: Loss converged well; train-val gap minimal (0.023 vs 0.025)
runs.run_insight: Best run in project with loss=0.023 and val_loss=0.025 at 1299 steps. Grad norm=2.27 is healthy. Should be resumed from checkpoint.

runs.name: mg13b_wan_dual_20260305_173822 (runs/mg13b_wan_dual_20260305_173822)
runs.signal_reason: Good progress over 16.5h with loss=0.043 before crash
runs.viz_insight: Loss decreasing; val_loss=0.029 shows generalization
runs.run_insight: Ran 2634 steps over 16.5h. Higher loss than flow_dual but reasonable given different architecture (wan_dual). Step time 2.2s suggests full GPU utilization.

projects.project_insight: All 7 runs crashed despite accumulating 105+ GPU-hours. The mg_flow_dual variants achieve lower loss (0.015-0.019, val ~0.024) than stack_d0d1 variants (0.06-0.10). The 14B model is 7x slower per step (3.2s vs 0.46s) with worse loss. Recommend focusing on 1.3B mg_flow_dual and fixing crash root cause.

runs.name: mg_flow_dual_128x256_20260307_1501 (2026-03-07/15-01-27)
runs.signal_reason: Best mg_flow_dual loss=0.015 but crashed after only 3h
runs.viz_insight: Loss=0.015, val=0.024 at 8599 steps; converging
runs.run_insight: Fastest convergence in mg_flow_dual series reaching loss=0.015 at 8599 steps in 3h. Crashed too early—would benefit from longer training.

runs.name: stack_d0d1_dual_128x256_N6_M4_1.3B_resume_20260307_1430 (2026-03-07/14-30-44)
runs.signal_reason: Best val_loss=0.020 in project despite higher train loss
runs.viz_insight: Val_loss=0.020 is best; train_loss=0.062 shows regularization effect
runs.run_insight: Resumed 1.3B stack model ran 16.6h reaching val_loss=0.020 (best in project). Train loss=0.062 is higher than mg_flow but better generalization. Step time 0.46s is efficient.

runs.name: stack_d0d1_dual_128x256_N6_M4_14B_20260303_1759 (2026-03-03/18-00-23)
runs.signal_reason: 14B model: 19h but loss=0.103, 7x slower step time
runs.viz_insight: Loss stuck at 0.103; vis_time 83s is very slow
runs.run_insight: 14B model used 19h but achieved only loss=0.103—5x worse than 1.3B mg_flow. Step time 3.2s and vis_time 83s are extremely slow. Not worth the compute cost.

runs.name: mg_flow_dual_128x256_20260306_1502 (2026-03-06/15-03-25)
runs.signal_reason: Longest run at 21.5h, loss=0.018, val=0.023
runs.viz_insight: Slight improvement over jb768h4y; plateau region
runs.run_insight: Longest mg_flow_dual run at 21.5h. Loss=0.018 and val=0.023 are marginally better than the 19.5h variant. Diminishing returns at this point.

runs.name: mg_flow_dual_128x256_20260306_1704 (2026-03-06/17-05-17)
runs.signal_reason: Solid 19.5h run with loss=0.019, val=0.024
runs.viz_insight: Converged to loss plateau around 0.019
runs.run_insight: Ran 19.5h reaching loss=0.019 at 2249 steps. Val_loss=0.024 shows small generalization gap. Good but not the best val_loss in this project.

runs.name: stack_d0d1_ego_N4_M4_1.3B_20260302_2016 (2026-03-02/20-17-14)
runs.signal_reason: Ego model trained 16.6h to loss=0.103
runs.viz_insight: Loss plateaued around 0.103; 16K steps completed
runs.run_insight: Ego variant ran longest in stack series at 16.6h. Loss=0.103 is comparable to 14B model but with much faster steps (0.45s). Ego formulation may inherently limit loss reduction.

runs.name: stack_d0d1_dual_N4_M4_1.3B_20260303_0844 (2026-03-03/08-44-38)
runs.signal_reason: 1.3B stack model at loss=0.060 after 8h
runs.viz_insight: Loss=0.060 after 3149 steps; consistent with stack architecture
runs.run_insight: Stack 1.3B model ran 8h to loss=0.060. Consistent with other stack models. Step time 0.87s is reasonable for this architecture.

projects.project_insight: Large-scale 3D geo-diffusion with diverse experiments. full_allattr (33h, finished) achieves best flow_mse=0.076. The LCuCpy chalcopyrite run consumed 56h but still has high total_loss=0.632 due to lithology complexity (25 active classes). MU3D resolution comparison shows 48x48x32 converges faster than 60x60x40. Several runs crashed from likely SLURM timeouts.

runs.name: LCuCpy-lith-cu-chalcopyrite-60x60x40-p4x4x4-cz19-8g-bs256-eff2048-lr1e-4-260307-0223
runs.signal_reason: 56h spent but total_loss=0.632 still high—most expensive run
runs.viz_insight: flow_mse=0.108; 25.6 active lithology classes adds complexity
runs.run_insight: Chalcopyrite model ran 56.4h reaching 3227 epochs. Total_loss=0.632 is high due to lithology complexity (25.6 active classes, majority_frac=0.11). Flow_mse=0.108 lags behind full_allattr. Consider if this task needs more capacity or different architecture.

runs.name: full_allattr_60x60x40-full-60x60x40-p4x4x4-cz34-8g-bs16-eff128-lr1e-4-260307-0205
runs.signal_reason: Best flow_mse=0.076, finished full training in 33h
runs.viz_insight: Fully converged with best metrics in project
runs.run_insight: Full all-attribute model is the top performer: flow_mse=0.076, total_loss=0.324, completed 1199 epochs in 33h. The larger batch (1152 global batches/epoch) and comprehensive attribute set work well.

runs.name: xpred-onehot33d-bs1024-0305-1630
runs.signal_reason: xpred model at loss=0.049, crashed at 21h
runs.viz_insight: Loss=0.049, boundary_frac=0.23; different task formulation
runs.run_insight: Cross-prediction model with onehot 33D encoding. Loss=0.049 and xpred_mse=0.096 at 1376 epochs. Boundary fraction=0.23 is an interesting metric. Crashed after 21h—needs restart.

runs.name: MU3D-48x48x32-uncond-48x48x32-p4x4x4-cz34-4g-bs32-acc1-eff128-lr1e-4-ep300-ds20260228-080947-r1-260303-0815
runs.signal_reason: MU3D 48x48x32 finished all 299 epochs, flow_loss=0.138
runs.viz_insight: Completed training; flow_loss=0.138 converged
runs.run_insight: 48x48x32 resolution completed full 299 epochs in 18h. Flow_loss=0.138 is higher than the 60x60x40 long run (0.109) but training completed successfully. Lower resolution converges faster but may have less capacity.

runs.name: MU3D-60x60x40-uncond-60x60x40-p4x4x4-cz34-4g-bs16-acc1-eff64-lr1e-4-ep300-ds20260228-080947-r1-260303-0815
runs.signal_reason: MU3D 60x60x40 ran 17.7h to flow_loss=0.109
runs.viz_insight: flow_loss=0.109 with 212 epochs; steady convergence
runs.run_insight: Higher resolution 60x60x40 model trained 17.7h. Flow_loss=0.109 is better than initial runs but slower to converge than 48x48x32 due to 2x more batches per epoch.

runs.name: lithology_with_CU-lith-cu-only-60x60x40-p4x4x4-cz18-8g-bs128-eff1024-lr1e-4-260307-0213
runs.signal_reason: Finished in 15.5h, total_loss=0.349 for lith+CU only
runs.viz_insight: Converged; flow_mse=0.134 is moderate
runs.run_insight: Lithology with CU-only finished at total_loss=0.349 in 1199 epochs. Flow_mse=0.134 is higher than full_allattr (0.076), suggesting the CU-only formulation is harder or needs tuning.

runs.name: LCU60_FINAL_8G_BS128_LR4E4-uncond-60x60x40-p4x4x4-cz19-8g-bs128-acc1-eff1024-lr4e-4-ep1000-ds20260228-080947-r1-260304-0308
runs.signal_reason: Final LCU60 run reached flow_loss=0.150 in 6h before crash
runs.viz_insight: flow_loss=0.150 improving from 0.566 baseline; conditional helps
runs.run_insight: LCU60 final model with 8 GPUs reached flow_loss=0.150 in 293 epochs (6h). Much better than the unconditional variant (0.566), confirming conditioning is essential.

runs.name: MU3D-60x60x40-uncond-60x60x40-p4x4x4-cz34-4g-bs16-acc1-eff64-lr1e-4-ep300-ds20260228-080947-r1-260302-2015
runs.signal_reason: 60x60x40 finished at 26 epochs, flow_loss=0.444
runs.viz_insight: Finished but only 26 epochs; still in early training
runs.run_insight: Completed its short schedule (26 epochs) in 3.6h. Flow_loss=0.444 is expected for so few epochs. Epoch time 415s is the slowest of all MU3D variants.

runs.name: LCI60-SCRATCH-uncond-60x60x40-p4x4x4-cz19-8g-bs512-acc1-eff4096-lr4e-4-ep1000-ds20260228-080947-r1-260305-1625
runs.signal_reason: Unconditional scratch model crashed at 5h with high flow_loss=0.566
runs.viz_insight: flow_loss=0.566 is very high; unconditional learning is slow
runs.run_insight: Unconditional model from scratch with large batch (bs512). Flow_loss=0.566 after 239 epochs shows unconditional generation from scratch is extremely challenging. 5h runtime was insufficient.

runs.name: MU3D-60x60x40-uncond-60x60x40-p4x4x4-cz34-4g-bs16-acc1-eff64-lr1e-4-ep300-ds20260228-080947-r1-260302-2354
runs.signal_reason: Crashed at 3.7h with flow_loss=0.185 at epoch 61
runs.viz_insight: Early training phase; loss was decreasing
runs.run_insight: 60x60x40 early crash at epoch 61. The longer retry (459qxo5z) ran much further. Epoch time 279s is comparable.

runs.name: MU3D-48x48x32-uncond-48x48x32-p4x4x4-cz34-4g-bs32-acc1-eff128-lr1e-4-ep300-ds20260228-080947-r1-260302-2354
runs.signal_reason: Early crash at 3.7h, 75 epochs with flow_loss=0.235
runs.viz_insight: Training was progressing (flow_loss=0.235 from ~0.49 start)
runs.run_insight: Predecessor to the successful c3c6psrw run. Crashed at epoch 75 but the retry completed. Epoch time 226s is much slower than the final run (85s), suggesting config was improved.

runs.name: MU3D-48x48x32-uncond-48x48x32-p4x4x4-cz34-4g-bs32-acc1-eff128-lr1e-4-ep300-ds20260228-080947-r1-260302-2015
runs.signal_reason: Earliest crash at 3.6h, only 34 epochs, flow_loss=0.491
runs.viz_insight: Very early training; loss still at initialization level
runs.run_insight: First attempt at 48x48x32, crashed at epoch 34 with flow_loss=0.491. Epoch time 340s is 4x the final run's 85s, suggesting the later runs had significant optimizations.

projects.project_insight: Active experimentation with 9 runs. both_6C_6T and mimic_only_6C_6T show strong loss (~0.03) while scratch_mimicgen has higher loss (0.10). The allegro_multiview finished run (18.9h) reached loss=0.084 at 20K steps. The flow-based approaches outperform pixel-space methods.

runs.name: allegro_multiview (2026-03-04/18-15-05)
runs.signal_reason: Only finished run: 18.9h to loss=0.084 at 20K steps
runs.viz_insight: Converged to loss=0.084; completed full 20K schedule
runs.run_insight: The only run to complete its full schedule (20K steps, 18.9h). Loss=0.084 is significantly better than the crashed 9.8h variant (0.139). Worth evaluating qualitatively.

runs.name: both_6C_6T (2026-03-09/01-33-57)
runs.signal_reason: Best loss=0.032 combining both data sources at 3724 steps
runs.viz_insight: Loss=0.032; competitive with mimic_only despite more diverse data
runs.run_insight: Combined both datasets achieving loss=0.032 in 4.4h. Slightly better than mimic_only (0.036), suggesting the combined data helps. Epoch=0 counter may indicate data loading issue.

runs.name: mimicgen_flow_all (2026-03-08/18-09-31)
runs.signal_reason: Longest flow run at 11.8h, loss=0.036 at 13K steps
runs.viz_insight: Loss plateau at ~0.036; likely converged
runs.run_insight: Mimicgen flow over all data for 11.8h. Loss=0.036 matches mimic_only despite 3x more steps, suggesting the model converged and additional data provides diversity but not loss improvement.

runs.name: allegro_multiview (2026-03-04/18-21-36)
runs.signal_reason: Allegro multiview at loss=0.139 after 9.8h, 12K steps
runs.viz_insight: Loss=0.139; higher than flow models, pixel-space is harder
runs.run_insight: Allegro multiview pixel model at loss=0.139 after 12K steps. Higher loss than flow-based variants reflects the difficulty of pixel-space generation.

runs.name: mimic_only_6C_6T (2026-03-09/01-22-05)

projects.project_insight: Systematic architecture and data fraction sweep completed. PreAct-18 dominates CCT (95.0% vs 86.5% at frac=1.0). Scaling from frac=0.2 to 1.0 boosts CCT eval_acc from ~72% to ~85% (entry) and ~86.5% (full). PreAct-18 with frac=0.2 achieves 87.4%—better than full-data CCT. All runs finished successfully.

runs.name: preact18--w1.0--frac0.2--ss1--seed1--entry-tl0.5-ag85--noreg
runs.signal_reason: PreAct-18 with only 20% data achieves 87.4%—beats full-data CCT
runs.viz_insight: Train=100%, eval=87.4%; strong despite limited data
runs.run_insight: PreAct-18 with only 20% data (2K samples) achieves 87.4% eval_acc—surpassing full-data CCT (86.86%). This demonstrates PreAct-18's superior data efficiency and architectural advantage.

runs.name: preact18--w1.0--frac1.0--ss52--seed2--entry-tl0.5-ag85--noreg
runs.signal_reason: Top eval_acc=95.03% with PreAct-18, seed=2
runs.viz_insight: Converged; train_acc=99.99% shows near-perfect fit
runs.run_insight: PreAct-18 achieves 95.03% eval accuracy with perfect training accuracy. Consistent with other seeds (94.92-95.10%). Clear winner in this architecture comparison.

runs.name: preact18--w1.0--frac1.0--ss52--seed1--entry-tl0.5-ag85--noreg
runs.signal_reason: Highest overall: PreAct-18 eval_acc=95.10%, seed=1
runs.viz_insight: Fully converged at 200 epochs
runs.run_insight: Best individual run across all architectures: 95.10% eval accuracy. PreAct-18 seed=1 is the top performer.

runs.viz_insight: Top entry performer; entry epoch=168
runs.run_insight: Best CCT entry result at 85.54% eval accuracy. Still 9.5% below PreAct-18, confirming architecture gap.

projects.project_insight: 18 runs with massive instability—most crash at epoch 0 or fail at launch. The longest run (drake_allegro, 11.2h) is the only productive one. The mimicgen flow+track linearization experiments had 10+ failed attempts before finding a working config. Fundamental setup issues need resolution before investing more compute.

runs.name: jacobian/mimicgen/dpt_M_128/flow/linearize_1 (2026-03-04/22-02-46)
runs.signal_reason: 10.2h mimicgen flow run failed—significant compute wasted
runs.viz_insight: Very small grad norms (~0.001); learning is extremely slow
runs.run_insight: Mimicgen flow (not flow+track) ran 10.2h but with extremely small gradients. The flow-only variant may need different LR or loss scaling.

runs.name: jacobian/drake_allegro/dpt_M_128/flow/linearize_1 (2026-03-08/09-17-07)
runs.signal_reason: Longest run at 11.2h—only one with meaningful training
runs.viz_insight: Grad norms declining (0.07→0.003 for decoder); learning happening
runs.run_insight: Drake allegro DPT-M 128 flow linearization ran 11.2h. Gradient norms are small but non-zero, suggesting training progresses slowly. This is the reference run for this project.

runs.name: jacobian/mimicgen/dpt_M_128/flow+track/linearize_1 (2026-03-04/17-21-56)
runs.signal_reason: 3.9h mimicgen flow+track run with small but non-zero grads
runs.viz_insight: Grad norms ~0.003-0.03; training progressing slowly
runs.run_insight: Second longest mimicgen run at 3.9h. Gradients are small but present. The flow+track formulation seems more trainable than flow-only.

runs.name: jacobian/mimicgen/dpt_M_128/flow+track/linearize_1 (2026-03-07/03-07-44)
runs.signal_reason: Mimicgen flow+track ran 3.1h with non-zero gradients
runs.viz_insight: Some decoder gradients are 0—possible dead neurons or frozen layers
runs.run_insight: Mimicgen flow+track linearization ran 3.1h. Some decoder layer 0 gradients are exactly 0, which is concerning—may indicate frozen parameters or dead neurons.

projects.project_insight: Clean hyperparameter sweep over hidden dimensions (h64→h1024) with 4 trials each. FID scales predictably: h768 achieves best FID=61.19, while h1024 is slightly worse at 63.36 (possible overfitting). h512→h256→h128→h64 show monotonic degradation. The h768 sweet spot suggests model capacity is sufficient at this size.

runs.signal_reason: h768 trial3: FID=61.44, second best overall
runs.viz_insight: Near-optimal hidden size for this architecture
runs.run_insight: h768 trial3 at FID=61.44, close to the best h768 trial (61.19). Loss=0.0013.

runs.signal_reason: Best overall: h768 trial1 FID=61.19
